as far as I know there is no public repo that has SLES packages available, binary or source.  getting them requires a subscription. It is possible to get evaluation copies of installable .ISO images at suse.com, I believe.

building server for sles12 on master, including ldiskfs, requires the following mod to be applied: http://review.whamcloud.com/15825 .   At this time it is still in flight, not yet landed.

Patch Set 2:

It looks to me like that opensuse.org location is a possible place to get valid SLES kernel sources after all.  I think if you pull the tag rpm-3.12.44-52.18 of the SLE12 branch from there you can get kernel source that exactly matches the most current released version of SLES 12 kernel source.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

The patch is ok, but suffers from one serious problem - in case of detecting on-disk corruption, using ext4_warning() instead of ext4_error() it no longer marks the filesystem superblock with an error so it automatically runs e2fsck before the next mount.  

That means the filesystem could remain corrupted until the admin manually unmounte it and forces a full check. Since this is no longer a fatal error, the admin may not notice this.

This will also cause problems because the free block counts are not being reduced by the number of free blocks in the corrupted group. That means the OST may report a large number of free blocks but not actually be able to allocate any blocks. That would confuse the MDS since it will continue to allocate objects there while free space is reported.

To fix this, there should be a helper function that reduces the free blocks count, sets the error flag in the superblock and marks the bitmap corrupt. 

Also, patches like this should be submitted to the linux-ext4 list for review and upstream landing in parallel, so that we don't need to patch ldiskfs forever.
Uploaded patch set 4.

parents.subject: LU-7315 osd-ldiskfs: handle pdo lock properly

commit.subject: LU-1026 ldiskfs: make bitmaps corruption not fatal
commit.message:
LU-1026 ldiskfs: make bitmaps corruption not fatal

We still hit bitmaps problems for rhel6 series kernel,
corruptions happen because ext4_mb_check_ondisk_bitmap()
check failed and FS become RO again:

ldiskfs_mb_check_ondisk_bitmap: on-disk bitmap for group
	294corrupted: 20180 blocks free in bitmap, 20181 - in gd
Aborting journal on device dm-6-8.
LDISKFS-fs (dm-6): Remounting filesystem read-only
ldiskfs_mb_new_blocks: Updating bitmap error: [err -30]
 [pa ffff880d9d6e4d68] [phy 14974976] [logic 8192] [len 3072]
 [free 3072] [error 1] [inode 278678]
ldiskfs_ext_new_extent_cb: Journal has aborted

this might be caused by some ext4 internal bugs, this patch
did the following things:

1.Inside ext4_read_block_bitmap() have gaven reasons
why it failed, so caller don't need call ext4_error() again.
2. mark block group corrupt and use ext4_warning() instead
of ext4_error().

There are still some bitmaps corruption places not handling,
let's keep it for now, and if it really hurt, let's add the
same handling codes logic later.

Tested by following scripts:

TEST_DEV="/dev/sdb"
TEST_MNT="/mnt/ext4"

mkdir -p $TEST_MNT
mkfs.ext4 -F $TEST_DEV >&/dev/null

mount -t ldiskfs $TEST_DEV $TEST_MNT
dd if=/dev/zero of=$TEST_MNT/largefile
	oflag=direct bs=10485760 count=200
umount $TEST_MNT
dd if=/dev/zero of=$TEST_DEV bs=4096 seek=641
	count=10 oflag=direct
mount -t ldiskfs $TEST_DEV $TEST_MNT
rm -f $TEST_MNT/largefile
dd if=/dev/zero of=$TEST_MNT/largefile oflag=direct
	bs=10485760 count=200 && echo
  "FILESYSTEM still usable after bitmaps corrupts happen"
dmesg | tail
umount $TEST_MNT
e2fsck $TEST_DEV -y

Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Iabb6ebf719d80d9ba4f41bee0b237e304212832b
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16679
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
